---
title: "correlationgraph"
output: html_document
---

# import data

```{r}
depression <- readRDS("train_original.rds")
str(depression)
```

```{r}
library(ggcorrplot)   # or use corrplot

# Convert all factors to numeric codes
dep_num <- data.frame(lapply(depression, function(x) {
  if (is.factor(x)) as.integer(x) else x
}))

# Compute Spearman correlation matrix
cor_mat <- cor(dep_num, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# Option 1: ggcorrplot (ggplot2-based, recommended)
ggcorrplot(cor_mat,
           method = "square",
           type = "full",         # or "lower" / "upper" for triangle
           lab = TRUE,            # show correlation values
           lab_size = 2.5,
           colors = c("#436db5", "white", "#c03030"),
           tl.cex = 9,
           title = "Spearman Correlation Matrix")

```
